preview_text:
The model favors Senegal at 61%, compared to Iraq's 12.5% win probability. Senegal enters as a clear favorite, though the model assigns Iraq a modest but non-negligible chance of an upset on the neutral ground.

The 27% draw probability suggests this match carries meaningful competitive balance despite the gap in win chances. The spread between Senegal (61%) and a draw (27%) indicates the model expects Senegal to control play, but Iraq's defensive capacity or set-piece threat may produce a stalemate result more often than a Senegal victory alone would imply. The outcome space remains reasonably distributed rather than heavily concentrated in one scenario.

The market consensus is considerably more bullish on Senegal than the model. Sportsbooks place Senegal at 72%, a gap of 11 percentage points, while assigning Iraq only 9.7% — nearly 3 points lower than the model's estimate. Polymarket prices are even more extreme, with Senegal at 74%. The dashboard does not adjudicate which view is more accurate; the divergence likely reflects differing assessments of squad strength or match conditions.
